description: Decoding fails or silently drops data when JSON keys, optionality, or types do not match the Codable model.
body: Failure mode: a Decodable model expects a key/type that differs from the payload (snake_case vs camelCase, a number sent as a string, a missing field declared non-optional), so decode throws keyNotFound/typeMismatch or the model misrepresents the data. Detection signals: DecodingError.keyNotFound/typeMismatch/valueNotFound at runtime; fields unexpectedly nil; works for one API response shape but not another. Do not assume the payload matches the struct exactly. Safer fallback: set keyDecodingStrategy = .convertFromSnakeCase or add CodingKeys, make genuinely-optional fields Optional, implement init(from:) for irregular shapes, and add a test decoding a real sample payload.
